semanticCore: Inspiration, instilling, causing to swallow instinctively, or divine suggestion

keyDerivations.meaning: To inspire, instil instinctively, suggest divinely, or cause to receive knowledge

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَنَفْسٍ وَمَا سَوَّاهَا ۞ فَأَلْهَمَهَا فُجُورَهَا وَتَقْوَاهَا (91:7-8): And by the soul and He who proportioned it, and inspired it with its wickedness and its righteousness.
summaryEn: The root signifies instilling thoughts or instinctive inspiration (Lahm), describing how God equipped the human soul with the inherent capability to distinguish good from evil.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر إلقاء الشيء في النفس وإلهامها سلوكاً معيناً بطريق الفطرة أو الغريزة (لهم)، مبيناً تعريف الله للنفس البشرية طريق التقوى والفجور غريزياً.
rawText: أَلْهَمَهُ ٱللّٰهُ لِلْخَيْرِ 4 [God directed him by inspiration to that which was good, or to prosperity] (TA, art. وفق) === أَلْهَمَهُ إِيَّاهُ He suggested it to him; (in the order of the words, he suggested to him it;) he put it into his mind. اِلْتَهَمَ 8 He gulped. لَاهُمَّ and اللّٰهُمَّ: see اللّٰهُ.
